name: Pepetech Affiliate + Directory Platform
description: Launch affiliate directory platform with first 10 tool reviews, Pinterest integration, SEO-ready pages indexed by Google
gate: 10 published tool reviews with affiliate links, Pinterest account live with pin templates, directory pages indexed

userStories.title: Decide: standalone domain vs pepewebtech.com/directory
userStories.description: As the business owner, I need to decide where Pepetech lives so the architecture and SEO strategy can be planned.
acceptanceCriteria: Decision documented: standalone domain (pepetech.com) OR subdirectory (/tools/ or /directory/)
If standalone: domain registered and DNS configured
If subdirectory: Next.js routing plan documented
Decision considers: SEO authority sharing, brand separation, maintenance cost
Mr. Z approves decision
userStories.notes: RECOMMENDATION: subdirectory (/tools/) to share SEO authority with pepewebtech.com. Standalone domain better for eventual sale.

userStories.title: Design directory taxonomy (categories, subcategories, tags)
userStories.description: As a content strategist, I need a clear taxonomy for the tool directory so content is organized and discoverable.
acceptanceCriteria: Top-level categories defined (min 5, max 10): AI Tools, Web Hosting, SEO, Marketing, Productivity, etc.
Subcategories for each top-level category (2-5 each)
Tag system for cross-cutting attributes (pricing tier, SMB-focused, bilingual, free-trial, etc.)
Taxonomy saved as JSON schema in project
Taxonomy supports SEO-friendly URL structure: /tools/{category}/{tool-name}
userStories.notes: Reference: G2.com, Capterra, Product Hunt category structures. Focus on small business relevance.

userStories.title: Build directory framework: category pages + tool detail pages
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the directory page templates built so tool reviews and category listings can be published.
acceptanceCriteria: Category listing page: grid of tools with name, logo, rating, price range, 'Visit' CTA
Tool detail page: hero section, pros/cons, feature list, pricing tiers, screenshots, affiliate CTA button
Both pages use Next.js SSG for performance
Responsive design (mobile-first)
Breadcrumb navigation: Home > Category > Tool
Schema.org markup: Product, Review, AggregateRating
userStories.notes: Build as Next.js pages. Use existing pepewebtech-website project if subdirectory approach.

userStories.title: Build affiliate link management system (redirect + tracking)
userStories.description: As a revenue operator, I need tracked affiliate links so I know which referrals convert and earn commissions.
acceptanceCriteria: Affiliate links use /go/{slug} redirect pattern (not raw affiliate URLs)
Redirect endpoint tracks: click timestamp, source page, referral tool, UTM params
Click data stored in lightweight DB (SQLite or Supabase)
Admin dashboard shows: clicks per link, clicks per tool, top referrers
Links are nofollow for SEO compliance
301/302 redirect configurable per link
userStories.notes: Keep it simple. SQLite for now, migrate to Supabase if traffic warrants. Use Next.js API routes for redirect.

userStories.title: Join first 3 affiliate networks
userStories.description: As a monetization operator, I need active affiliate accounts so I can earn commissions on tool referrals.
acceptanceCriteria: Amazon Associates account approved (easiest entry)
Impact.com or ShareASale account approved (software/SaaS focused)
One additional network joined (PartnerStack, CJ, or direct programs)
Affiliate IDs/links documented in secure config (not in git)
Test affiliate links: click → verify tracking cookie set
userStories.notes: Amazon Associates: instant approval for most sites. Impact/ShareASale: 1-7 day review. Some programs require minimum traffic.

userStories.title: Write first 10 tool reviews with affiliate links
userStories.description: As a content creator, I need the first 10 in-depth tool reviews published so the directory has launch content.
acceptanceCriteria: 10 reviews published covering: AI tools (3), web hosting (2), SEO tools (2), marketing (2), productivity (1)
Each review: 1000-2000 words, pros/cons, feature table, pricing, verdict
Each review has affiliate CTA button with tracked link
Each review has hero image (generated via Z-Image-Turbo)
Each review has schema markup (Product + Review JSON-LD)
Reviews published and accessible via directory pages
userStories.notes: Use Hermes blog pipeline as template. Each review is a detailed content piece, not thin affiliate page.

userStories.title: Design + build comparison table component (X vs Y)
userStories.description: As a content creator, I need a reusable comparison component so I can publish 'Tool A vs Tool B' posts that rank for comparison searches.
acceptanceCriteria: Comparison page template: side-by-side feature table, pricing comparison, pros/cons, verdict
Component accepts JSON data for the two tools being compared
Mobile-responsive: tables scroll horizontally on small screens
Schema markup: Product comparisons for rich results
Example comparison: 'Jasper vs Copy.ai' or 'Shopify vs WooCommerce' published
userStories.notes: Comparison posts are highest SEO value for affiliate content. 'X vs Y' search intent is commercial (ready to buy).

userStories.title: Design + build roundup template (Top 10 X for Y)
userStories.description: As a content creator, I need a roundup template so I can publish 'Top N Tools for X' posts that maximize affiliate link density.
acceptanceCriteria: Roundup page template: numbered list with tool cards (name, logo, 2-sentence pitch, pros, pricing, CTA)
Template supports 5-20 items per roundup
Each item has affiliate CTA with tracked link
Intro paragraph optimized for target keyword
Example roundup: 'Top 10 AI Tools for Small Business in 2026' published
userStories.notes: Roundups are the bread and butter of affiliate sites. Each item = one affiliate link opportunity.

userStories.title: Pinterest business account setup + pin template design
userStories.description: As a traffic acquisition operator, I need a Pinterest business account with pin templates so I can drive visual traffic to directory pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Pinterest business account created or converted from personal
5 boards created matching directory categories
Pin templates designed: product review pin, comparison pin, roundup pin, tip pin
Each template: 1000x1500px (Pinterest optimal), bold text, brand colors
Templates saved as ComfyUI workflows or Canva templates for easy reuse
userStories.notes: Pinterest drives 33% of referral traffic for many affiliate sites. Visual platform = perfect for tool screenshots + graphics.

userStories.title: Pinterest automation workflow (auto-pin on publish)
userStories.description: As a content operator, I need pins auto-created and scheduled when new reviews/roundups are published so Pinterest stays active without manual work.
acceptanceCriteria: On publish of new review: auto-generate pin image using template
Pin auto-posted to relevant Pinterest board with SEO description
Pin links back to the review/roundup page
Scheduling: stagger pins across the week (not all at once)
Pin performance tracked (impressions, clicks, saves)
userStories.notes: Pinterest API for business accounts. Or use Tailwind/Buffer for scheduling. Cron job via Hermes.

userStories.title: SEO strategy: schema markup for reviews (Product, Review, AggregateRating)
userStories.description: As an SEO operator, I need proper structured data on all review/directory pages so Google shows rich results (star ratings, prices, availability).
acceptanceCriteria: Product schema on every tool detail page with name, description, offers
Review schema with author, date, rating, review body
AggregateRating schema with average rating and count
FAQ schema on comparison pages (if applicable)
All schemas validated with Google Rich Results Test (no errors)
BreadcrumbList schema on all directory pages
userStories.notes: Rich results = higher CTR in search. Star ratings in SERPs can increase CTR by 20-30%.

userStories.title: Hero images + category thumbnails via local Z-Image-Turbo
userStories.description: As a content creator, I need branded hero images and category thumbnails generated locally so the directory looks professional at $0 image cost.
acceptanceCriteria: Hero images for each category page (5-10 images)
Default tool thumbnail for tools without logos (placeholder)
Comparison infographic images for X vs Y posts
Roundup header images for Top N posts
All images in WebP format, under 200KB each
Consistent visual style across all generated images
userStories.notes: Uses Phase 1 Z-Image-Turbo local gen. Brand presets from P1-007 apply here.

userStories.title: Content pipeline: automated affiliate content generation
userStories.description: As a content operator, I need a cron job that researches and drafts new tool reviews so the directory grows without manual work.
acceptanceCriteria: Cron job runs weekly: research trending tools → draft review → add to review queue
Research includes: features, pricing, pros/cons, competitors, user reviews
Draft review saved as markdown for human review before publish
Draft includes suggested affiliate programs for the tool
Queue system: draft → reviewed → approved → published
userStories.notes: Extend existing blog pipeline cron. Same pattern: research → draft → QC → publish. Different content type.

userStories.title: Analytics: affiliate click tracking + revenue dashboard
userStories.description: As a business operator, I need to see which tools drive clicks and revenue so I can optimize the directory for earnings.
acceptanceCriteria: Click tracking from P3-004 feeds into analytics
Dashboard shows: total clicks, clicks per tool, clicks per category, top performers
Revenue data imported from affiliate network dashboards (manual or API)
Monthly revenue report auto-generated
Dashboard accessible via admin route or local file
userStories.notes: Start simple: SQLite + static HTML dashboard. Upgrade to real-time later.

userStories.title: Launch: first 50 directory pages live + submitted to Google
userStories.description: As the launch operator, I need 50 pages live and indexed so the directory starts receiving organic search traffic.
acceptanceCriteria: 50 pages published: 10 reviews + 5 comparisons + 3 roundups + category pages + tool detail stubs
XML sitemap includes all directory pages
Google Search Console submitted with sitemap URL
All pages return HTTP 200
All pages have unique title + meta description
Core Web Vitals: LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1, FID < 100ms
userStories.notes: 50 pages = critical mass for Google to start taking the directory seriously. Quality over quantity.
